name: Bambi (Bayesian Model Building Interface)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Tomás Capretto and contributors

performance.notes: PyMC backend for MCMC; NUTS sampler; speed depends on model complexity and chain length

agent_readiness.notes: R-like formula syntax for Bayesian regression via PyMC backend. import bambi as bmb. model = bmb.Model('y ~ x1 + x2', data=df). idata = model.fit(draws=1000). model.predict(idata, kind='pps'). bmb.interpret.plot_predictions(model, idata, covariates=['x1']). Supports: linear, logistic, Poisson, mixed effects. Formula: 'y ~ x1 + (1|group)' for random effects. Auto priors via PyMC. ArviZ for diagnostics. Designed for social science / applied statistics. Much simpler than raw PyMC for regression models.

gotchas: PyMC dependency — inherits PyMC's installation complexity
Limited to regression-family models (not general probabilistic programs)
Formula syntax R-compatible but Python teams may find it unfamiliar
Slower than frequentist alternatives (statsmodels) due to MCMC
best_when: Bayesian regression with R-like formula interface
Applied statistics researchers transitioning from R to Python
When PyMC's raw API is too verbose for standard regression
avoid_when: Custom probabilistic models outside regression family (use PyMC directly)
Teams unfamiliar with Bayesian inference or R formula syntax
summary: Bambi provides R-like formula syntax for Bayesian regression models via PyMC. bmb.Model('y ~ x1 + (1|group)', data=df).fit(draws=1000). Linear, logistic, Poisson, mixed effects with formula specification. Auto priors, ArviZ diagnostics. plot_predictions() for interpretation. Much simpler than raw PyMC for standard regression. Best for applied statistics and social science teams wanting Bayesian regression with R-familiar interface.
